source_key.External: Grounded in a regulatory framework or recognised external standard
source_key.Portfolio: No external standard covers this concept; IDX is the originating authority, grounded in portfolio traffic evidence
source_key.Market: Market convention or best practice without formal regulatory backing

nodes.name: Climate strategy and targets
nodes.definition: Overarching climate commitments and the strategic logic connecting them to the business model.

nodes.name: Greenhouse gas emissions
nodes.definition: Quantified Scope 1, 2, and 3 emissions data with methodology, boundaries, and year-on-year trend.

nodes.name: Climate transition and resilience
nodes.definition: Scenario analysis of physical and transition climate risks and the organisation's adaptation plans.

nodes.name: Energy use and efficiency
nodes.definition: Total energy consumption, intensity, and operational measures to reduce energy use.

nodes.name: Renewable energy
nodes.definition: Renewable energy sourcing, generation, procurement mechanisms, and progress against targets.

nodes.name: Carbon markets and offsets
nodes.definition: Use of voluntary and compliance carbon markets, offset purchases, and removal credits.

nodes.name: Nature and biodiversity
nodes.definition: Impacts, dependencies, and commitments relating to natural ecosystems, species, habitats, and nature-positive ambitions.

nodes.name: Water stewardship
nodes.definition: Management of water as a shared resource — consumption, withdrawal, discharge, and watershed engagement.

nodes.name: Waste and circular economy
nodes.definition: Minimising waste generation, maximising resource recovery, and designing for circularity.

nodes.name: Plastics and packaging
nodes.definition: Commitments, targets, and progress on reducing plastic use and redesigning packaging for recyclability or reuse.

nodes.name: Pollution and hazardous materials
nodes.definition: Management of pollutants, hazardous substances, and emissions to air, soil, and water beyond GHG.

nodes.name: Workforce health, safety and wellbeing
nodes.definition: Safety management systems, incident data, and wellbeing programmes for the workforce.

nodes.name: Diversity, equity and inclusion
nodes.definition: Workforce diversity data, DEI targets, and the equity of opportunity and inclusion within the organisation's culture.

nodes.name: Human rights and labour standards
nodes.definition: Due diligence processes for respecting human rights and upholding labour standards across operations and value chain.

nodes.name: Community and social impact
nodes.definition: Relationship with and contribution to the communities in which the organisation operates.

nodes.name: Livelihoods
nodes.definition: Contribution to the economic security of individuals and households in the value chain, particularly smallholders.

nodes.name: Consumer and stakeholder engagement
nodes.definition: Active engagement with consumers and external stakeholders on ESG matters, including green claims governance.

nodes.name: Responsible products and services
nodes.definition: Ensuring products and services are safe, ethically produced, and designed to deliver positive outcomes.

nodes.name: ESG governance and oversight
nodes.definition: Board and executive structures and accountabilities for overseeing ESG strategy, risk, and performance.

nodes.name: Business ethics and anti-corruption
nodes.definition: Policies and systems for preventing bribery, corruption, fraud, and anti-competitive behaviour.

nodes.name: Tax transparency
nodes.definition: Tax strategy, governance, and public reporting including country-by-country payments.

nodes.name: Supply chain and suppliers
nodes.definition: Management of supplier relationships with respect to ESG standards, due diligence, and supply chain transparency.

nodes.name: Policy and regulatory engagement
nodes.definition: Engagement with governments and regulators on ESG-relevant policy, including lobbying transparency.

nodes.name: ESG reporting and disclosure
nodes.definition: Approach to producing, publishing, and assuring ESG information including materiality assessment.

nodes.name: Framework alignment
nodes.definition: Explicit mapping of ESG disclosures to recognised external standards — the cross-reference layer.

nodes.name: ESG targets and progress
nodes.definition: Quantified ESG targets with timescales and current progress — ambition versus delivery in one view.

nodes.name: ESG ratings and indices
nodes.definition: Participation in and performance against major ESG rating systems and sustainability indices.

nodes.name: Results and financial performance
nodes.definition: Periodic reporting of financial results — revenue, profit, margins, cash flow, and KPIs with narrative context.

nodes.name: Annual report and accounts
nodes.definition: The primary statutory financial report covering full-year statements, strategic report, and governance disclosures.

nodes.name: Results presentations and webcasts
nodes.definition: Presentation decks, webcast recordings, and transcripts providing narrative context on top of statutory results.

nodes.name: Regulatory announcements and filings
nodes.definition: Formal regulatory disclosures including RNS announcements, inside information notifications, and exchange filings.

nodes.name: Financial outlook and guidance
nodes.definition: Forward-looking statements on expected financial performance including formal guidance ranges and assumptions.

nodes.name: Capital allocation
nodes.definition: Framework for deploying capital across investment, shareholder returns, debt, and M&A.

nodes.name: Dividends and buybacks
nodes.definition: Policy and activity relating to cash returns to shareholders — dividends, special dividends, and buyback programmes.

nodes.name: Debt and fixed income
nodes.definition: Information for debt investors — bond issuance, credit ratings, debt maturity profiles, and green bond frameworks.

nodes.name: M&A and corporate transactions
nodes.definition: Mergers, acquisitions, disposals, and demergers — deal rationale, terms, and post-completion updates.

nodes.name: Corporate strategy
nodes.definition: Medium and long-term strategic direction — growth priorities, strategic pillars, and transformation programmes.

nodes.name: Business model and value creation
nodes.definition: How the organisation creates, delivers, and captures value — inputs, activities, outputs, outcomes.

nodes.name: Markets and competitive position
nodes.definition: The markets the organisation operates in, its competitive positioning, and the structural trends shaping its opportunity.

nodes.name: Investment case
nodes.definition: The explicit articulation of why the organisation represents a compelling investment opportunity.

nodes.name: Board composition and effectiveness
nodes.definition: Composition, skills, independence, and effectiveness of the board — biographies, tenure, and committee membership.

nodes.name: Executive remuneration
nodes.definition: How executive directors are paid — policy, pay outcomes, LTIP awards, and pay-performance alignment.

nodes.name: Shareholder rights and AGM
nodes.definition: AGM documentation, proxy voting, resolutions, and the mechanisms through which shareholders exercise ownership rights.

nodes.name: Risk management and principal risks
nodes.definition: Framework for identifying and managing risk — principal risks, risk appetite, viability, and internal controls.

nodes.name: Regulatory and legal matters
nodes.definition: Significant regulatory investigations, litigation, and compliance matters representing material risks.

nodes.name: Corporate governance framework
nodes.definition: The full governance framework as a navigable entry point — committee terms of reference, policies, and comply-or-explain statements.

nodes.name: Investor events and presentations
nodes.definition: Capital markets days, results webcasts, conference appearances, and the full archive of investor event content.

nodes.name: Shareholder centre
nodes.definition: Consolidated hub for retail and institutional shareholder services — share price, register, dividend history.

nodes.name: Analyst consensus and estimates
nodes.definition: Published external analyst consensus estimates for key financial metrics.

nodes.name: ESG for investors
nodes.definition: IR-domain expression of ESG — how ESG data and credentials are packaged for the investment community.

nodes.name: Investor contacts and financial calendar
nodes.definition: IR team contact details, forward financial calendar, and email alert services.

nodes.name: Debt investor centre
nodes.definition: Dedicated section for fixed income investors — bond documentation, credit ratings, and fixed income contacts.

nodes.name: Press releases and announcements
nodes.definition: Official statements issued to media and the public — the primary output of the press office function.

nodes.name: News articles and editorial
nodes.definition: Authored content produced by the organisation in a news or editorial format.

nodes.name: Media contacts and enquiries
nodes.definition: The operational interface between the organisation and the media — press contact details and enquiry routing.

nodes.name: Multimedia library
nodes.definition: Repository of approved visual and audio assets for press and media use.

nodes.name: Purpose and values
nodes.definition: The organisation's statement of why it exists beyond commercial purpose and the values that govern it.

nodes.name: Company history and heritage
nodes.definition: Founding story, key milestones, and historical development providing narrative context for current identity.

nodes.name: Leadership and executive team
nodes.definition: Profiles of senior leadership — biographies, roles, and accountability for named executives.

nodes.name: Corporate story and about us
nodes.definition: Consolidated self-description — what the company does, where it operates, and what makes it distinctive.

nodes.name: Crisis and issues communication
nodes.definition: Management of public communication during periods of reputational stress — crisis statements and issue responses.

nodes.name: Corporate positions and statements
nodes.definition: Formally stated positions on matters of public interest — social issues, political developments, industry debates.

nodes.name: Regulatory and policy communication
nodes.definition: External communication on regulatory matters and public policy engagement — the communication output of policy work.

nodes.name: Stakeholder correspondence and transparency
nodes.definition: Published responses to stakeholder enquiries, FOI disclosures, and other transparency mechanisms.

nodes.name: Corporate campaigns and initiatives
nodes.definition: Discrete, named corporate communications campaigns designed to shift awareness, perception, or behaviour.

nodes.name: Thought leadership and opinion
nodes.definition: Content in which the organisation or its executives advance a perspective on topics relevant to the business.

nodes.name: Awards and recognition
nodes.definition: External awards, rankings, accreditations, and recognitions received — third-party validation signals.

nodes.name: Corporate partnerships and sponsorship
nodes.definition: Formal partnership and sponsorship relationships — commercial, charitable, academic, and industry.

nodes.name: Social media presence
nodes.definition: Owned social media channels and the content published through them — including official profile listings.

nodes.name: Corporate website and digital estate
nodes.definition: Owned digital properties — primary corporate website, microsites, regional sites, and their governance.

nodes.name: Content strategy and editorial calendar
nodes.definition: Forward publishing plan and editorial governance — content themes, cadence, and standards.

nodes.name: Supplier engagement and procurement
nodes.definition: Operational communication with the supplier base — how to become a supplier, procurement processes, and supplier portal access.

nodes.name: Job listings and search
nodes.definition: Published vacancy listings — the primary interface through which candidates discover open roles.

nodes.name: Application process and guidance
nodes.definition: Explanation of how to apply — recruitment process stages, what to expect, and support available.

nodes.name: Graduate and early careers
nodes.definition: Programmes and pathways for graduates, school leavers, apprentices, and early career candidates.

nodes.name: Experienced hire and specialist recruitment
nodes.definition: Recruitment content targeting mid-career, senior, and specialist candidates.

nodes.name: Why work here
nodes.definition: The consolidated headline EVP — what makes this a distinctive and compelling place to work.

nodes.name: Rewards and benefits
nodes.definition: Published information on compensation, benefits, and financial rewards available to employees.

nodes.name: Flexible and hybrid working
nodes.definition: Policies and narrative around flexible working — hybrid, remote, part-time, and other non-standard patterns.

nodes.name: Learning and development
nodes.definition: Investment in employee learning, skills development, and career growth.

nodes.name: Culture and ways of working
nodes.definition: Articulation of workplace culture — behaviours, norms, and ways of working that define the employee experience.

nodes.name: Diversity, equity and inclusion in talent
nodes.definition: DEI commitments and programmes as they relate specifically to the talent and employment experience.

nodes.name: Wellbeing and mental health
nodes.definition: Support for employee wellbeing — mental health resources, physical wellbeing, and financial wellbeing.

nodes.name: Employee communities and networks
nodes.definition: Employee-led or employer-supported networks and communities forming part of the inclusive culture proposition.

nodes.name: Mission and purpose at work
nodes.definition: The connection between corporate purpose and the individual employee experience — working here as meaningful.

nodes.name: Social and environmental impact through work
nodes.definition: How the organisation enables employees to contribute to social and environmental impact through their roles.

nodes.name: Innovation and technology careers
nodes.definition: Careers content targeting technology, data, digital, and innovation candidates.

nodes.name: Life at the organisation
nodes.definition: Immersive content giving prospective candidates a direct window into the day-to-day working experience.

nodes.name: Locations and global opportunities
nodes.definition: Global footprint as a talent proposition — where roles are based and the opportunity to work internationally.

nodes.name: Awards and employer recognition
nodes.definition: External recognition as an employer — best employer awards, certifications, and ranking appearances.

nodes.name: Brand portfolio overview
nodes.definition: Consolidated presentation of the full brand estate — all brands owned, managed, or licensed.

nodes.name: Individual brand pages
nodes.definition: Dedicated pages for individual brands — identity, product range, positioning, and consumer entry points.

nodes.name: Brand heritage and history
nodes.definition: Historical narrative of individual brands — founding stories, evolution, and cultural significance.

nodes.name: Product and service catalogue
nodes.definition: Navigable catalogue of all products and services — the primary interface for product discovery.

nodes.name: Product innovation and pipeline
nodes.definition: New product development, innovation pipeline, and R&D activity communicated as a brand story.

nodes.name: Product sustainability and responsibility
nodes.definition: Sustainability credentials and responsible sourcing claims at the product level.

nodes.name: Markets and geographies
nodes.definition: The markets, geographies, and customer segments the organisation serves.

nodes.name: Customer and partner channels
nodes.definition: Routes through which customers and partners access products and services.

nodes.name: Brand positioning and competitive differentiation
nodes.definition: What makes the organisation's brands and products distinctively valuable — the competitive USP.

nodes.name: Brand guidelines and identity
nodes.definition: Published guidance on how brands should be represented — visual identity standards for external audiences.

nodes.name: Intellectual property and licensing
nodes.definition: Communication of the IP position — trademarks, patents, and licensing terms for brand assets and technologies.

nodes.name: Product certifications and standards compliance
nodes.definition: External certifications, quality standards, and regulatory compliance credentials held by products.

nodes.name: Consumer brand content
nodes.definition: Brand and product content formats specific to consumer-facing organisations — recipes, inspiration, community.

nodes.name: B2B and industrial product content
nodes.definition: Brand and product content formats specific to B2B and industrial organisations — technical specs, case studies.

nodes.name: Operations and business units
nodes.definition: The organisation's operational footprint — where it operates, what it does at each location or business unit, and the scale of its activities.
